prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response:
The lighthouse keeper trudged up the narrow, spiral staircase, guided by the dim, flickering glow of his oil lamp. The shift change at this desolate, forgotten outpost was always bleary-eyed, but tonight, the thick, unrelenting fog that rolled in from the ocean made it feel as though the very air itself had congealed to envelop him.

Unlocking the heavy door to the gallery, he stepped out onto the enclosed platform, the metal below his boots wet and slick with the morning's mist. The beam of his lantern fell on the mighty lighthouse lamp, its Fresnel glass panels glistening in the damp air. He reached for the brass switch to extinguish the powerful bulb, signaling the end of another sleepless night.

That's when he saw it.

In the sweeping arc of the unlit beam, a shape emerged, illuminated by the ethereal, pre-dawn glow. A small, weathered dinghy, wedged against the rocks far below. He dropped the lantern, its meager light extinguished as it shattered on the rocks below.

For a moment, He froze, the sound of waves crashing against the weathered stones the only company to his disbelief. The lighthouse phone line had been down for weeks; he was alone.

Taking a deep, frigid breath, the lighthouse keeper descended the stairs, two at a time, each step more urgent than the last. He dove into the frigid, dark water, his heart pounding as much from exertion as it did from the unknown horrors—or perhaps, joys—he might discover upon reaching the unexpected visitor on this day, in this place, shrouded in the unyielding embrace of the lonely, ever-present fog.
